title: Create a new project from the dashboard
description: This verifies that a user can enter a project name on the dashboard and open a new project in the editor.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill in the project name field with a valid name
Submit the project creation form
Verify the editor is displayed
Verify an active scene is available for editing

title: Drag an imported asset onto the canvas
description: This verifies that a user can drag a local asset from the sidebar onto the canvas and create a visible canvas element.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Open the local folder picker from the asset sidebar
Select a local folder containing supported media files
Drag one asset from the sidebar onto the canvas
Verify a new canvas element is displayed
Verify the asset remains available in the sidebar

title: Move and resize a canvas element while keeping state in sync
description: This verifies that a placed canvas element can be repositioned and resized and that the editor continues to reflect the updated element state.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Open the local folder picker from the asset sidebar
Select a local folder containing supported media files
Drag one asset from the sidebar onto the canvas
Select the canvas element
Move the selected element on the canvas
Resize the selected element on the canvas
Verify the element remains visible in its updated position and size
Verify the editor still shows the selected element as editable

title: Edit an element visibility window from the timeline
description: This verifies that a user can add an element, see it appear in the per-scene timeline, and adjust its visibility window with the timeline handles.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Create a new project from the dashboard
Add a text or image element to the active scene
Open the element timeline
Adjust the element visibility window using the timeline handles
Verify the element track is displayed in the timeline
Verify the element visibility window is updated

title: Split text into individual character elements
description: This verifies that a creator can convert a text element into separate character elements for granular editing.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the lettering entry point in the header
Click the control to add a text element
Type text into the text field
Click the control to split the text into individual letters
Verify the text is broken into individual character elements on the canvas

title: Create lettering and split it into character elements
description: This verifies that a user can open the lettering workflow, add styled text, and split it into individual character elements.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Open the lettering panel
Enter text for a lettering element
Configure the typography and animation settings
Add the lettering element to the canvas
Split the lettering into characters
Verify the lettering appears on the canvas
Verify individual character elements are displayed

title: Add a new scene and switch to it
description: This verifies that a user can create an additional scene and make it the active scene so the canvas updates accordingly.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Create a new project from the dashboard
Add a new scene
Select the newly added scene
Verify the selected scene is active
Verify the canvas updates for the active scene

title: Import a local asset folder into the sidebar
description: This verifies that a user can pick a local folder and see supported media files appear in the asset sidebar.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Open the local folder picker from the asset sidebar
Select a local folder containing supported media files
Verify the imported assets are listed in the sidebar
Verify unsupported files are not shown

title: Add and reorder scenes with pauses and transitions
description: This verifies that a user can build a multi-scene essay by adding scenes, inserting active pauses, reordering scenes, and switching between transition types.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Add a new scene
Add an active pause
Add another new scene
Reorder the scenes
Change the transition type between scenes
Verify the scene list reflects the new ordering
Verify the pause and transition controls are present

title: Reorder scenes in the timeline
description: This verifies that a user can drag scenes to a new position and see the scene order update in the timeline.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Create a new project from the dashboard
Add two new scenes
Reorder the scenes in the scene list
Verify the scene order is updated
Verify the active scene remains available

title: Edit the visibility window of a scene element in the timeline
description: This verifies that a user can adjust an element's start and end timing in the scene timeline so its visibility window changes.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Add a scene element to the canvas
Open the per-scene timeline
Drag the start handle of the element timing bar
Drag the end handle of the element timing bar
Verify the element timing window is updated in the timeline
Verify the element remains associated with the scene

title: Open lettering panel and add a text element
description: This verifies that a creator can open the lettering workflow and place a new text element on the canvas.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the lettering entry point in the header
Click the control to add a text element
Verify a new text element is displayed on the canvas

title: Toggle the transition between two scenes
description: This verifies that a user can switch the transition type between scenes and see the transition state update.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Create a new project from the dashboard
Add a new scene
Toggle the transition between the two scenes
Verify the transition type is updated
Verify the scene boundary reflects the new transition

title: Configure text typography and animation
description: This verifies that a creator can adjust typography settings and choose a text animation for a text element.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the lettering entry point in the header
Click the control to add a text element
Type text into the text field
Change the font family using the dropdown
Adjust the font size using the slider
Pick a text color using the color picker
Select a text animation effect
Verify the text element remains displayed on the canvas with the configured appearance

title: Prevent adding text without an active scene
description: This verifies that the app blocks text creation when no active scene is available and prompts the user to create or select one.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the lettering entry point in the header
Click the control to add a text element
Verify a prompt to select or create an active scene is visible
Verify no text element is added to the canvas

title: Add a pause block and preview it on canvas
description: This verifies that a user can add an active pause and preview the pause state on the canvas.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Create a new project from the dashboard
Add an active pause using a black screen option
Select the pause block
Verify the pause is displayed in the scene list
Verify the canvas shows the pause preview

title: Keep lettered text available after closing and reopening the panel
description: This verifies that a created text element remains on the canvas after the lettering panel is dismissed and reopened.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the lettering entry point in the header
Click the control to add a text element
Click the lettering entry point in the header again
Click the lettering entry point in the header once more
Verify the previously added text element is still displayed on the canvas

title: Block project creation without a name
description: This verifies that the dashboard prevents creating a project when the name field is empty.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Leave the project name field empty
Submit the project creation form
Verify a required-name validation message is visible
Verify the editor is not displayed

title: Add a VHS pause and see the animated canvas state
description: This verifies that a user can create a VHS-style active pause and see the distinctive animated pause preview on the canvas.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Create a new project from the dashboard
Add an active pause using a VHS option
Select the pause block
Verify the pause is displayed in the scene list
Verify the canvas shows the VHS pause preview

title: Create multiple text elements for different lettering work
description: This verifies that a creator can add more than one text element while working in the lettering panel.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the lettering entry point in the header
Click the control to add a text element
Click the control to add another text element
Verify multiple text elements are displayed on the canvas

title: Show an empty timeline when a scene has no elements
description: This verifies that a user sees the expected empty state when opening a scene without any placed elements.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Create a new project from the dashboard
Verify the scene has no elements
Verify the timeline panel is hidden or shows an empty state message

title: Reject an unsupported file dropped on the canvas
description: This verifies that dropping an unsupported file type onto the canvas does not create a usable canvas element.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Drop an unsupported file type onto the canvas
Verify no new canvas element is added
Verify a rejected-file message is visible

title: Confirm pause duration remains fixed after creation
description: This verifies that a user cannot change an active pause duration after it has been created and that the duration remains fixed.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Create a new project from the dashboard
Add an active pause using a solid color option
Select the pause block
Attempt to edit the pause duration
Verify the pause duration cannot be modified
Verify the fixed pause duration remains in effect

title: Handle canceling the folder picker
description: This verifies that canceling folder selection leaves the asset sidebar unchanged.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Open the local folder picker from the asset sidebar
Cancel the folder selection
Verify no new assets are added to the sidebar
Verify the empty folder state remains visible
